mnemonics: Deep Learning (DL) & Reinforcement Learning (RL) are like a student (agent) learning from a giant textbook (environment) with a tough grader (reward function) to get good grades (maximize cumulative reward).
keyPoints: Reinforcement Learning involves an agent learning to make decisions by performing actions in an environment to maximize cumulative reward.
Deep Learning provides the function approximation (e.g., through neural networks) that allows RL to scale to complex problems with high-dimensional state spaces (like video games).
The combination of Deep Learning and Reinforcement Learning, known as Deep Reinforcement Learning, was famously used by DeepMind to create agents that mastered games like Go and Atari.
commonMistakes: Confusing the objective of RL (maximizing long-term cumulative reward) with supervised learning (mapping inputs to known outputs).
Underestimating the sample inefficiency and instability of training deep reinforcement learning agents compared to other ML paradigms.
